_comment: External MCP servers Nexus will spawn on startup. Each entry: {name, command (shell-split), env (optional), enabled (optional, default true), skip_if_missing_env (optional, default true — skip if any env value is empty), allowed_tools (optional list — when set, only tools in the list are exposed; everything else is filtered out, used to enforce read-only scope)}. Tools appear in Nexus as '<name>__<tool>'.

servers.name: github
servers.note: Fill GITHUB_TOKEN (and GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN) with a fine-grained PAT. The entry auto-skips while the values are empty so Nexus boots cleanly without a token.

servers.name: mcp_fs
servers.note: Phase 16.10 — read-only filesystem access scoped to ~/AI_Agent and ~/Dev. Write tools (write_file, edit_file, create_directory, move_file) are intentionally excluded; enabling writes is a separate dispatch.

servers.name: mcp_excel
servers.note: Phase 16.10 — read-only Excel access. Write/format/delete/insert tools are excluded. Server reads any .xlsx path passed in by the caller; no path scoping at the server level.

servers.name: mcp_obsidian
servers.note: Phase 16.10 — disabled. No Obsidian vault present at ~/Obsidian on WattBott, and mcp-obsidian requires Obsidian's Local REST API plugin running with an API key. Re-enable after: (1) install Obsidian, (2) create vault at ~/Obsidian, (3) install Local REST API plugin, (4) set OBSIDIAN_API_KEY in .env, (5) flip enabled=true.
